actual.name: actual
nodes: three-point anchor: pursuit, undone threads, vision
Markov and Heisenberg as skeleton constraints
actual.summary: A system that receives a user's half-formed concept, unfinished inspirations, and visualized vision, then uses that three-point anchor to send AI agents wandering unpredictably across domains — math, physics, theater, literature, anything — to surface native and metaphorical work that can advance the user's concept, either by building on existing published work or by articulating a vague new idea into something live and actionable.

node_records.text: three-point anchor: pursuit, undone threads, vision
node_records.embedding_text: a user's half-baked current pursuit, unresolved inspirational threads, and internally visualized end-state, taken together as a structural anchor for search
autonomous agents that traverse knowledge domains without a predetermined path, surfacing unexpected structural matches
finding both literal domain matches and cross-domain structural analogues to a user's problem across science, art, and culture
taking existing published research or prior work as a base and identifying the next layer of development that has not yet been articulated
a system that converts a user's inarticulate internal vision into a structured, livable concept by finding external resonances
using unpredictability and non-linear traversal as a feature rather than a bug, so that agent paths cannot be forecast but can be tracked after the fact

node_records.text: Markov and Heisenberg as skeleton constraints
node_records.embedding_text: probabilistic state-transition logic and the principle of partial observability used not as full theories but as structural shapes that govern how the system moves and what it can know about itself

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: The core tension is between the need for a stable anchor — something the wandering must stay answerable to — and the need for genuine unpredictability in the path taken, because predictable wandering produces only what the user already knows. The system must be free enough to find the unexpected and constrained enough to return something useful. A second tension: the user's vision is real but inarticulate — it exists as a felt shape, not a sentence — and the system must treat that felt shape as a valid, load-bearing input rather than waiting for the user to fully articulate before searching. The third dynamic is inheritance vs. origination: the system must serve both the person who wants to extend a known lineage and the person who wants to break from it entirely, without collapsing both into the same mode.

node_records.embedding_text: a system that is genuinely unpredictable in its path but remains answerable to a fixed structural reference point — chaos that does not drift into uselessness
treating a user's inarticulate but real internal sense of direction as a legitimate and sufficient starting signal, rather than requiring full verbal articulation before proceeding
the incompleteness of the user's concept is not a deficiency to fix before the system can run — it is the generative condition the system is designed to operate within
a process whose history is fully observable and recordable but whose next state cannot be predicted — the value lives in the trail, not the forecast

search_queries: building on prior work vs original contribution
extension versus departure in creative systems
node_records.embedding_text: the system must serve both the mode of extending an existing lineage and the mode of departing from it entirely, without flattening both into the same operation
the system is not controlled by predicting or directing the agent's path, but by observing what it returns and scoring it against the anchor — control through evaluation, not prescription

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: constraint at output layer, not path layer, preserves discovery value
anchor as scoring surface, not destination
partial input preserves search space width
structural analogy as cross-domain retrieval primitive
non-prediction as a design requirement, not a limitation
mechanism.summary: The causal primitive here is: when the target output is a genuinely novel connection that the user could not have found by directed search, the system cannot use directed search — it must use structurally constrained randomness. The anchor is not a destination; it is a scoring surface. The agents wander freely, and the anchor scores what they return. This means the constraint operates at the output layer, not the path layer — which is the only configuration that preserves the unpredictability that makes the discovery valuable. A second causal shape: partial information at input (the user's vague vision) is not noise to be filtered but signal to be preserved, because full articulation would collapse the search space to what the user already knows. The system is therefore causally dependent on the user NOT fully knowing what they want.

node_records.text: constraint at output layer, not path layer, preserves discovery value
search_queries: novelty preservation in search system design
node_records.embedding_text: systems designed to find what directed search cannot find must leave the path unconstrained and apply constraint only at the evaluation of what is returned — path-level constraint destroys the output's novelty

node_records.text: anchor as scoring surface, not destination
node_records.embedding_text: the anchor does not tell the system where to go — it tells the system how to score what comes back, functioning as a resonance detector rather than a navigation target

node_records.text: partial input preserves search space width
search_queries: partial query advantages in discovery systems
node_records.embedding_text: a fully articulated user intent collapses the search space to already-known territory; partial articulation keeps the space open, making the user's incompleteness a structural asset rather than a deficiency

node_records.text: structural analogy as cross-domain retrieval primitive
search_queries: dynamic shape matching in knowledge systems
node_records.embedding_text: the system retrieves not by matching surface vocabulary but by matching the dynamic shape of a problem — any domain that exhibits the same forces, tensions, and constraints is a valid match regardless of topic

node_records.text: non-prediction as a design requirement, not a limitation
node_records.embedding_text: the inability to predict where the agent goes next is not a failure of the system — it is the mechanism by which the system can return something the user could not have predicted wanting

a single signal (current work, or inspiration, or vision alone) produces an anchor too narrow or too vague to score against; three signals from different temporal and epistemic positions triangulate a stable scoring surface

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I'm currently working on a system that helps people advance their concepts or the work they're currently working on. If there's a case or research that's already published, based on that inheritance, based on that work, we can advance it. If I'm the one who didn't want to be at the same level and I'm just having some similar inspirations and I wanted to take that method or research paper or concept to a next level — how can I do this? I'm making the system like that. Or I'm having a whole new concept: if there's a system I'm building and I wanted to make a feature out of it and I'm having some vague inspirations of that system but I don't know how to articulate it, I don't know how to make it live — there's a system that can do that part of it.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: If a person tells the inspiration, the pursuit, and any unfinished work or unfinished threads in their mind — some of the big inspirations — to the model, then the model has something to anchor against. These three information points — the current pursuit (whether half-baked or not), the undone inspirations, and the vision the user is actually visualizing in their brain — can give enough data to the model to keep intact its score and keep wandering around through the Internet to find native and metaphorical work in the respective domains, whether it's math, physics, psychology, or even theater, literature, anything.

current_map.name: current_map
current_map.content: Based on what I told you about this system, you can't be working on it without some theoretical seeds. I stumbled into many concepts — Heisenberg, Markov chain, chaos theory. Chaos theory is what lets the model wander the Internet unhindered, like total chaos. There's no prediction of where the model will go next, but we can track it. But that track is the past, not the future. We cannot predict where it will end up. That is the spontaneousness I want to give to the model. The Markov chain also plays an important role in my thinking. The same goes with the Heisenberg principle — it doesn't provide a clear understanding, but a half-understanding. Just a structure. Just a skeleton.
